records.ety: [OF. dihten, AS. dihtan to dictate, command, dispose, arrange, fr. L. dictare to say often, dictate, order; cf. G. dichten to write poetry, fr. L. dictare. See Dictate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dight or Dighted; p. pr. & vb. n. Dighting.]

defs: To prepare; to put in order; hence, to dress, or put on; to array; to adorn.
quotes: Two harmless turtles, dight for sacrifice.
The clouds in thousand liveries dight.
